channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Lawsuit Accuses Meta of Using AI to Target Workers With Medical Conditions for Layoffs
item.description: Twenty-six former employees of Meta Platforms have filed a lawsuit against the tech company, accusing it of using AI-powered software that disproportionately targeted people with disabilities or who took medical leave in selecting people for mass layoffs. The 26 plaintiffs, who filed the lawsuit anonymously, are accusing Meta of violating federal and state laws that ban discrimination or retaliation against workers who have disabilities, take medical leave or are pregnant. The lawsuit, filed in Oakland, California, federal court late Monday, says that the company relied on factors such as productivity and AI token usage when it began slashing thousands of jobs earlier this year, disadvantaging people who missed work because of medical conditions.

item.title: Keystone Pipeline System’s Operator to Pay $26.9M Penalty Over Kansas Oil Spill
item.description: government would require the Keystone Pipeline system’s operator to pay a $26.9 million civil penalty over a major oil spill in Kansas in December 2022 and spend about $40 million more to prevent future accidents. government, an engineering consulting firm said that a bend in the Keystone system where the spill occurred had been “overstressed” since its installation in December 2010 — likely because construction activity itself altered the land around the pipe. The accident was the largest onshore crude pipeline spill in the U.S.

item.title: Lawsuit Claims Meta Used AI to Target Workers with Medical Conditions for Layoffs
item.description: Twenty-six former employees of Meta Platforms have filed a lawsuit against the tech company, accusing it of using AI-powered software that disproportionately targeted people with disabilities or who took medical leave in selecting people for mass layoffs. The 26 plaintiffs, who filed the lawsuit anonymously, are accusing Meta of violating federal and state laws that ban discrimination or retaliation against workers who have disabilities, take medical leave or are pregnant. The lawsuit, filed in Oakland, California, federal court late Monday, says that the company relied on factors such as productivity and AI token usage when it began slashing thousands of jobs earlier this year, disadvantaging people who missed work because of medical conditions.

item.title: Florida Conducting Annual Slow Down Campaign to Target Speeders
item.description: The state of Florida announced its 9th annual Operation Southern Slow Down campaign targeting speeders across the state, according to a joint announcement by the Florida Department of Transportation (FDOT), the Florida Department of Highway Safety and Motor Vehicles (FLHSMV) and its division of the Florida Highway Patrol (FHP). The multi-state campaign happening across Alabama, Florida, Georgia, South Carolina, and Tennessee, takes place July 13-18, 2026, and focuses on reducing speed-related crashes through a combination of increased enforcement and public education. The choice is simple: slow down, obey the law, and make it home safe.”

item.title: Brute-Force Password Cracking Getting Faster, Longer Passwords Are Better
item.description: Password cracking is a brute-force numbers problem, and the chips designed for AI hold no advantage at it, the cybersecurity company said. New analysis reveals that password cracking becomes 20 to 25% faster every year, according to cybersecurity firm Hive Systems. Measured on the same hashing standard across all three years, the time to brute-force a randomly generated eight-character password using the full mix of characters has fallen from 225 years in 2024 to 164 in 2025 to 132 in 2026 – a drop of roughly 20 to 25% annually.

item.title: ARPC’s claim payouts up marginally in Q1 to surpass AU$1.5bn
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Insurance Industry May Be Unprepared for Agentic AI Risks
item.description: New research shows the insurance industry might not be equipped to deal with AI risks. As covered here earlier this month, the insurance industry and regulators have begun pricing AI hallucination risks. While some industry figures say these warnings are overblown, and part of a campaign to push new AI insurance products, AIUC Co-Founder Rajiv Dattani said legal liabilities had held back broader AI use at larger companies.

item.title: David Maslo appointed CEO of African Risk Capacity Ltd
item.description: African Risk Capacity Limited (ARC Ltd.) the financial affiliate and parametric insurance underwriting entity of the African Risk Capacity (ARC) Group, has appointed David Maslo as its permanent CEO, following his successful tenure as interim leader during a period of significant transformation for the company. It will also strengthen the continent’s resilience to climate-related, health and other catastrophic risks, and support Africa’s wider sustainable development agenda.” Commenting on his appointment, David Maslo, Chief Executive Officer of ARC Ltd, said: “It is an honour to lead African Risk Capacity Ltd at this important moment in Africa’s economic development. appointed Maslo as Interim CEO, following his tenure as Chief Risk Management Officer at the Caribbean Catastrophe Risk Insurance Facility Segregated Portfolio Company (CCRIF SPC).

item.title: Reinsurance sidecars remain key in 2026, as third-party capital deployment holds stable: Aon Securities
item.description: Reinsurance sidecars remain a key theme in 2026, with third-party capital deployment holding broadly stable despite a rapidly evolving landscape of perils and structures, according to Aon Securities, the broker-dealer and investment banking arm of the broking firm. “Sidecars remain a major theme in 2026, with overall capital deployment broadly stable and considerable investor interest in asset-driven sidecar vehicles for casualty and whole-account portfolios,” the firm said. Data from the firm showed that reinsurance sidecars reached a new record high of $17 billion as of June 30th 2025 , representing a 70% expansion of this segment of the ILS market in just one year, and marked a notable increased from Aon’s previous estimate that outstanding reinsurance sidecar capital had reached $10 billion at the middle of 2024.

item.title: Active Re closes 2025 with 88.4% CoR and total equity of $108m
item.description: Active Re, a global reinsurer headquartered in Barbados, has released its 2025 Annual Report “Positioned for What’s Ahead”, reporting a combined ratio of 88.4% for 2025, as well as a return on equity of 16.1%, a technical result of USD 26.7 million, and total equity of USD 108 million. Despite a global reinsurance market defined by abundant capital, pricing adjustments across several lines of business, and intensifying competition, Active Re maintained a strategy that prioritises portfolio quality over volume growth. For the third consecutive year, AM Best reaffirmed Active Re’s Financial Strength Rating of A (Excellent) and Long-Term Issuer Credit Rating of “a” (Excellent), both with a stable outlook, highlighting the company’s balance sheet strength, consistent operating performance, and robust enterprise risk management framework.

item.title: Guy Carpenter strengthens LatAm & Caribbean team with Almaraz and Genovese appointments
item.description: Based in Miami, Almaraz and Genovese bring a strong portfolio and deep expertise in health re/insurance that will enhance Guy Carpenter’s health reinsurance capabilities across Latin America and the Caribbean. Both executives join the broker from Worldwide Insurance Associates (WIA) – an international risk management and insurance agency specialising in the Latin American marketplace -, with Almaraz having spent 33 years working for the insurance brokerage Almaraz and Genovese are some of Guy Carpenter’s most recent appointments, following Dennis Sugrue’s as Head of International Business Intelligence. Guy Carpenter, a global reinsurance broker and Marsh business, has announced the appointment of James Almaraz and Robert Genovese as Managing Director and Vice President, respectively.

item.title: Fitch improves Canopius Re outlook to positive while affirming ‘A’ rating
item.description: Credit rating agency Fitch Ratings has upgraded the outlook for Canopius Reinsurance Limited (Canopius Re), the Bermuda-based reinsurance company within the Canopius Group, from Stable to Positive while reaffirming its Insurer Financial Strength (IFS) Rating at ‘A’ (Strong). Fitch also reaffirmed that the rating is supported by Canopius Re’s role as a core operating company within the group, together with the group’s capital position and consistently strong financial performance. While Fitch considers the implied IFS Rating to be ‘A+’, it has maintained the provisional rating at ‘A’.

item.title: Antares’ CEO Michael van der Straaten Dies at 58
item.description: Antares, the specialist global re/insurance group, announced the death of its chief executive officer Michael van der Straaten. “It is with great sadness that we announce that Michael van der Straaten, CEO of Antares, passed away unexpectedly this weekend following a short illness,” according to Antares, the international operations of Qatar Insurance Group (QIC), a major insurer in the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region. van der Straaten, 58, was named CEO of Antares (formerly named QIC Global ), in 2020.

item.title: AM Best Assigns Credit Ratings to Arkansas’ WoodStar Reciprocal Exchange
item.description: AM Best has assigned a Financial Strength Rating of A- (Excellent) and a Long-Term Issuer Credit Rating of “a-” (Excellent) to WoodStar Reciprocal Exchange (WoodStar), based in Little Rock, Arkansas. In addition, AM Best has assigned a Long-Term Issue Credit Rating of “bbb” (Good) to the $220 million, 9.5% fixed surplus notes, due 2041, issued by WoodStar. The ratings reflect WoodStar’s balance sheet strength, which AM Best assesses as very strong, as well as its adequate operating performance, neutral business profile and appropriate enterprise risk management (ERM).

item.title: Indiana Woman Pleads Guilty to Defrauding Elderly Victim of More Than $500K
item.description: An Indiana woman has pleaded guilty to wire fraud for her role in a six‑year scheme that targeted and financially devastated an elderly victim Shayna Williams, 51, of Westfield, was involved in a car accident with an elderly woman. In total, the victim paid Williams more than $500,000. Following the accident, over the course of six years, Williams defrauded the victim and the victim’s friend of $672,086 by repeatedly lying about injuries, medical conditions, and other expenses she claimed resulted from the car accident.

item.title: Feathery raises $30 million to expand AI operating system for financial services
item.description: AI workflow platform Feathery has announced $30 million in total funding , including a recently completed Series Around, as it accelerates development of its AI operating and decisioning platform for financial services. Building an AI operating system for financial services Feathery positions itself as an enterprise AI platform designed to automate and optimize operational workflows across highly regulated financial institutions. The company says it now serves  more than 300 financial services firms , including insurers, insurance brokers, registered investment advisers (RIAs), and wealth management firms.

item.title: Feathery raises $30 million
item.description: Data intake platform Feathery announced it has raised $30 million in total funding, including its recently completed Series A, from Portage Ventures, Index Ventures, Allstate Strategic Ventures, Clocktower Ventures, Erie Strategic Ventures, and Bain Capital Ventures. Founded in 2021, Feathery’s system collects and structures client information, synchronizes every major system of record, and normalizes data across all surfaces. Feathery turns those challenges into opportunities for each firm to sharpen their competitive edge, create personalized client experiences and scale operations.” – Peter Dun, co-founder and CEO of Feathery.

item.title: Allstate names Christian Lown CFO
item.description: Allstate has appointed Christian “Chris” Lown as executive vice president and chief financial officer, effective August 3. Lown joins Allstate from CoStar Group, where he served as CFO and oversaw finance, investor relations, business development and facilities. He will report to Allstate Chair, President and CEO Tom Wilson.

item.title: NFP acquires Total Benefits Advisors
item.description: NFP , an Aon company, has acquired Total Benefits Advisors, a Cleveland-based firm specializing in employee benefits, retirement services and wealth management. Founded in 2009, Total Benefits Advisors serves employers, business owners and high-net-worth individuals in Northeast Ohio. “We are excited to welcome Mark and the Total Benefits Advisors team to NFP as we grow our presence in the greater Cleveland market.

item.title: Joaquin Carlos Uy rejoins Howden Philippines as financial lines leader
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Dual rolls out Liberty-backed global transactional risk unit with Australia, New Zealand among initial markets
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Streamlined rulebook to save asset managers £128m a year
item.description: A large share of the £128m-a-year savings are expected to come from simpler Fund Reporting for Asset Management Entities (FRAME) requirements. Simon Walls, executive director, markets, at the FCA, said: 'By tailoring the regime for UK asset managers, we can collect better data while also saving industry 10s of millions of pounds a year. The FCA has proposed a package of reforms that would tailor requirements proportionately for asset managers, cut costs for firms and give better data to supervise the sector more effectively.

item.title: New member appointed to the Regulatory Decisions Committee
item.description: The FCA Board has appointed Dan Lavender as a new member of its Regulatory Decisions Committee (RDC). The RDC is responsible for taking certain regulatory decisions on behalf of the FCA relating to contested enforcement action. Committee members bring a broad range of professional experience to support fair, independent and evidence-based decision-making.

item.title: So Far, 2026 Is A Solid Year For Cybersecurity Startup Funding
item.description: Cybersecurity isn’t winning the war for attention in a startup investment landscape still dominated by megarounds for AI pioneers. Nonetheless, venture funding to the space is holding up at historically high levels in 2026. Those were the findings from an analysis of global funding for the first half of the year to companies in Crunchbase security- and privacy-related industry categories.

item.title: Kilter and Blue Owl capitalise new Accelerant Risk Exchange reciprocal insurer WoodStar
item.description: A new insurer has been launched supported by third-party capital from investors and asset managers KKR-backed Kilter Finance and Blue Owl Capital, with newly rated WoodStar Reciprocal Exchange set to provide dedicated underwriting capacity through the Accelerant Risk Exchange. WoodStar Reciprocal Exchange is backed by third-party capital from specialist and major investors, which include Kilter Finance, a firm that provides capital solutions to small and mid-sized insurers, and funds managed by alternative asset manager Blue Owl Capital, Accelerant said in announcing the partnership it has forged with WoodStar. WoodStar Reciprocal Exchange has received an ‘A-‘ financial strength rating from AM Best, while $220 million of 9.5% fixed surplus notes, due 2041, issued by the insurer have also been rated ‘bbb’.

item.title: Catastrophe bond market records that were set in Q2 2026
item.description: The catastrophe bond market reached new heights in terms of issuance and activity levels in the second-quarter of 2026, with a number of important records being set as cat bond sponsors and investors continued to show growing commitment to the space. Having published our new report that analyses second-quarter and first-half 2026 catastrophe bond issuance last week, we wanted to follow-up and highlight some of the new records that were set in Q2. Last week we delved into the half-year records that were broken in the cat bond market in H1 2026 , now we look at the quarter only and it is a similar story of benchmarks being raised and high-bars being reset higher still.

item.title: PERILS raises Victoria bushfire insured market loss estimate to AU$860m
item.description: Catastrophe data aggregator PERILS has published its third industry loss estimate for the Victoria Bushfires which took place during the period of January 7th to 13th, 2026, with the insured industry loss now standing at AU$860 million. Adding: “The bushfires were driven by extreme heat, dry vegetation, strong winds, and ignition sources such as lightning and human activity with around 900 buildings destroyed, disruption to critical infrastructure, and one fatality.” PERILS has confirmed that an updated estimate of the insurance market loss from the bushfires will be published on January 13th, 2027, twelve months after the event end date. PERILS published its initial industry loss estimate for the event in late February, six weeks after the event end date, putting the total at AU$786 million.

item.title: US P&C industry records strongest underwriting profit in a decade: AM Best
item.description: property and casualty industry recorded its largest underwriting profit and lowest combined ratio in a decade, driven by improved underwriting performance and pricing, according to a recent AM Best report. personal lines segment’s underwriting profit almost quadrupled to $45.7 billion in 2025 and comprised 51% of total industry direct premiums written, while the commercial lines segment’s profit more than doubled to $19.2 billion. David Blades, associate director at AM Best, said, “Insurers underwriting both personal auto and homeowners’ lines of coverage have reaped the benefits of technology and data analytics to supplement underwriting, claims handling and ratemaking.

item.title: African Risk Capacity appoints David Maslo as CEO
item.description: Africa’s first development-focused parametric insurer, African Risk Capacity Limited (ARC Ltd.), has handed Interim Chief Executive Officer (CEO), David Maslo, the role on a permanent basis, after he successfully led the firm through a period of significant transformation. It will also strengthen the continent’s resilience to climate-related, health and other catastrophic risks, and support Africa’s wider sustainable development agenda.” Maslo commented: “It is an honour to lead African Risk Capacity Ltd at this important moment in Africa’s economic development. appointed Maslo as Interim CEO in June 2025 , following his tenure as Chief Risk Management Officer at the Caribbean Catastrophe Risk Insurance Facility Segregated Portfolio Company (CCRIF SPC).

item.title: Saudi Re to strengthen Lloyd’s presence with 22.5% stake in AdA Risk
item.description: Saudi Reinsurance Company (Saudi Re) has received approval from the kingdom’s Insurance Authority to acquire a 22.5% equity stake in UK-based AdA Risk Holding Co Limited, the operator of AdA Syndicate at Lloyd’s, for £8.95 million. The acquisition will be financed from Saudi Re’s own financial resources, and is expected to support the reinsurer’s international expansion plans, and accelerate growth globally by strengthening its presence at Lloyd’s, the world’s oldest insurance and reinsurance marketplace. Saudi Re also expects the partnership to improve its ability to develop specialised reinsurance solutions, diversify its underwriting portfolio, and leverage the expertise, market access, and capabilities available within the Lloyd’s market.

item.title: People Moves: Clifford Takes On Expanded Role for NFP’s Construction Unit
item.description: Brokerage and Account Management Leader Tom Clifford assumed an expanded leadership role as leader of U.S. Based in New York, Clifford will oversee the C&I brokerage strategy and relationships with insurers and wholesalers, as well as brokers and account management personnel for all construction P&C lines. brokerage and account management in the construction and infrastructure (C&I) group at NFP , an Aon company.

item.title: Iran and US launch New Missile Attacks, Battle Over Control of Strait of Hormuz
item.description: air base in Jordan on Tuesday and the United States attacked Iranian targets for five hours in a battle for control of the Strait of Hormuz that has pushed up oil prices to four-week highs. Read more: Trump Says US Reinstates Blockade of Strait of Hormuz After New Clashes With Iran Regional analysts said the hostilities remained within controlled boundaries for now, with both sides seeking leverage for an eventual peace deal, but that there was still a risk of fighting spinning out of control. forces launched waves of attacks for the third successive night after Iran said on Saturday it was closing the strait, prompting U.S.

item.title: UAE Oil Tankers Attacked While Sailing Dark Through Hormuz
item.description: Two United Arab Emirates-linked supertankers came under attack while sailing “dark” through the Strait of Hormuz, threatening one of the main methods of getting non-Iranian crude to the global market during the war. They also involved two entities — the UAE and South Korean shipowner Sinokor Group — that have proven pivotal in transporting barrels through the strait over the past few months. The vessels have been performing “shuttle runs” with their transponders turned off to avoid detection, a practice that’s been going on for weeks and helped to significantly boost oil flows through the waterway that in normal times accounts for about a fifth of the world’s supply.

item.title: MSIG Asia appoints Ronak Shah as Regional CEO to lead expanded Asia-Pacific business
item.description: The post MSIG Asia appoints Ronak Shah as Regional CEO to lead expanded Asia-Pacific business appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Across more than 20 years in the insurance industry in Asia and North America, Shah has built experience in: Business transformation Specialty insurance development Regional growth strategies Customer and broker relationships Leadership across complex international markets Driving the next phase of growth In his new position, Shah will be responsible for: Setting MSIG Asia’s regional strategic direction Accelerating business performance across key markets Strengthening collaboration between regional operations Deepening relationships with customers and distribution partners Delivering long-term value creation across the Asia-Pacific business Leadership comments Hironori Morimoto, Group Chief Global Officer and Senior Managing Executive Officer of MS&AD, said Shah’s experience made him well positioned to lead the company’s next stage of development. MSIG Asia has appointed Ronak Shah as its new Regional Chief Executive Officer , effective October 1, 2026 , as the insurer strengthens its leadership structure and expands regional oversight across the Asia-Pacific market.

item.title: Flock launches Jay, an AI fleet insurance assistant for customers and brokers
item.description: Integrated into the Flock Portal , Jay is available at no additional cost to all Flock fleet insurance customers and brokers placing business with the insurer. Benefits for brokers The AI assistant is also being made available to insurance brokers that place business with Flock. “We believe this is the future of insurance: getting ahead of the problem, and helping customers prevent accidents before they even happen.” Klinger also noted that brokers receive the same AI capabilities for every fleet they manage, extending the technology beyond end customers.

item.title: Novacore selects FurtherAI as strategic AI partner to modernize underwriting operations
item.description: The post Novacore selects FurtherAI as strategic AI partner to modernize underwriting operations appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Insurance platform Novacore has selected FurtherAI as its strategic artificial intelligence partner, deploying the company’s AI workspace as the foundation for underwriting operations across multiple insurance programs. “FurtherAI is going to be a strategic lever as we continue to expand the Novacore workspace and pursue acquisitions.” He added that the platform provides a centralized intelligence layer that enables underwriting teams to spend less time searching for information and more time making underwriting decisions.

item.title: Lemonade expands renters insurance to Maine
item.description: Lemonade , which serves more than 3 million customers across the US, UK and Europe, has launched renters insurance in Maine, with coverage starting at $5 per month. Now we can deliver that to Maine, too.” – Dan Timsit, head of renters insurance at Lemonade. “Renters in Maine deserve the same easy insurance experience as everyone else.

item.title: Cover Genius raises $100 million
item.description: Embedded insurance company Cover Genius announced a $100 million capital raise, backed by Vista Credit Partners. Cover Genius operates a B2B2C embedded protection platform that connects 200+ partners with 50+ global insurance carriers to protect “over 70 million” end customers at the point of sale across travel, retail, ticketing and logistics. Excluding this investment round, Cover Genius publicly disclosed $245 million in funding.

item.title: Reliance launches AI agent for insurance back offices
item.description: Reliance Global Group has launched an AI agent that automates browser-based work across its insurance agency network. This agent allows us to absorb that work without scaling headcount while maintaining the controls required in insurance operations.” – COO Judah Korman. The agent cannot submit, issue or bind transactions.

item.title: El Gobierno relanza el Sandbox financiero para darle más agilidad y seguridad
item.description: El Gobierno trabaja en una reforma del Sandbox financiero con el objetivo de agilizar y facilitar la experimentación de proyectos innovadores. La medida se incluye en el Anteproyecto de Ley de Digitalización y Modernización del Sector Financiero, que será remitido esta semana al Congreso.

item.title: El 72% de los mediadores detecta más demanda de ciberseguros, pero un tercio no sabe explicarlos
item.description: El crecimiento del interés por los ciberseguros no está yendo acompañado de un aumento equivalente en la capacidad de los mediadores para comercializarlos.

item.title: RibéSalat cierra 2025 con un crecimiento del 24,5% en primas intermediadas
item.description: RibéSalat alcanzó el pasado ejercicio un volumen de primas intermediadas de 1.370 millones de euros, lo que supone un crecimiento del 24,5%.

item.title: Workers at Kentucky Candle Plant Not Limited to Comp Claims, Appellate Court Finds
item.description: Employees who were killed and injured when a powerful tornado struck a Kentucky candle factory in 2021 are not necessarily limited to the workers’ compensation exclusive remedy, a Kentucky appeals court said in a decision that could have far-reaching impact for employers and commercial liability insurers. The Kentucky workers’ compensation statute, like most states’ laws, allows an exception to the exclusive remedy and grand bargain of the comp system if an injury or death is caused by an employer’s deliberate, aggressive actions, the court noted. The Kentucky Court of Appeals found that the workers can sue Mayfield Consumer Products and a supervisor for damages because the employer had threatened the workers with termination and had unlawfully intimidated or restrained them from leaving the building before the EF-4 twister struck.

item.title: US Hits Iran With Strikes, Blockade as Trump Plans Hormuz Charge
item.description: New York time, the US had begun launching a “third consecutive night of strikes against Iran.” Trump had vowed the US would keep up attacks on Iran, saying in a radio interview that “we’re going to hit them very hard tonight, and we’re going to hit them hard tomorrow.” Later in the Oval Office, he told reporters the strikes were aimed at targeting Iran’s capabilities “for anything having to do with the strait.” Brent crude rose above $85 a barrel for the first time in a month after Trump reimposed the blockade and demanded payment for cargo moving through the strait. President Donald Trump reinstated the US blockade of Iranian ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz and demanded a 20% reimbursement for all other cargo shipped through the waterway. A reinstatement of the blockade on Iranian ports may prompt the Islamic Republic to step up attacks on ships seeking to transit the Strait of Hormuz, which would continue a pattern of tit-for-tat strikes over the past week.

item.title: Clash of Florida Titans Pits Powerful Tribe Against Homebuilder Lennar
item.description: But the Seminole tribe’s sovereign status has so far kept their fight in the courts, resulting in a highly visible clash between two powerful players in Florida’s real estate- and tourism-focused economy. The Seminole Tribe of Florida hired Miami-based home construction giant Lennar Corp. “We wanted to keep our community together.” Last year, the Seminoles sued Lennar, claiming defects in more than 500 homes built on six different reservations across Florida, including the one near Hollywood.

item.title: Sompo taps Canon Liu as head of financial lines for Hong Kong, Macau and Taiwan
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: APAC transaction liability transforms into ‘mature and established’ risk class: Aon
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: January Victoria bushfire losses rise to US$596m: Perils
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Ategrity expects $205m GWP with CoR under 87% for Q2’26
item.description: Ategrity expects to report record-breaking numbers across all core financial metrics for Q2 2026, driven by broad-based growth in both property and casualty lines. Additionally, Ategrity expects to see record diluted earnings per share of more than $0.60 in Q2 2026. Ategrity Specialty Insurance Company Holdings has announced its preliminary financial results for the second quarter of 2026, reporting gross written premiums (GWP) of more than $205 million and a combined ratio below 87%.

item.title: Rokstone adds Claire Lill to North American Casualty team
item.description: Rokstone, the international specialty re/insurance managing general agent (MGA), has announced the addition of Claire Lill to its North American Casualty team. Lill joins Rokstone’s established North American Casualty leadership team, which includes Larry Burrows, Ben Baker, Alex Fitzpatrick, and Lex Beesley. Larry Burrows, Head of North American Casualty at Rokstone, said, “Claire has one of the strongest reputations in the market and brings a great deal of discipline, technical expertise, and strong broker relationships to the wider team.
